biographical_info.force: Han, Sun Jian's Forces, Yuan Shu's Forces, Wu
biographical_info.weapon: Club and shield (3~5), Club (6~7, 9), Iron Boat (7:E, 8), Gauntlets (Origins)
biographical_info.battle: Yellow Turban Rebellion, Ou Xing's Rebellion, Sishui Gate, Hulao Gate, Xiangyang, Wujun, Chibi
biographical_info.footnotes: Possibly born in the years 145~147. Speculations for his death date range from 213~223.

sections.heading: Lead
sections.content:
Huang Gai (onyomi: Kō Gai) is a veteran Wu general who served three generations of the Sun family, starting with Sun Jian. He devised the plan to burn Cao Cao's fleet at Chibi under the guise of a false defection. He is saved by Han Dang after falling in the Yangtze river during the battle. Romance of the Three Kingdoms depicts Huang Gai as pivotal to the fire attack's success, but not as its inventor. He was posthumously heralded as one of the Five Elders of Wu.

Fans voted his Dynasty Warriors persona to fifty-sixth place in Gamecity's Dynasty Warriors 7 character popularity poll; he placed last in the poll for Dynasty Warriors 8: Xtreme Legends. The latest poll for the eighth installment puts him in twenty-seventh. The character poll for overseas fans puts him in seventeenth place for the Wu division.

sections.heading: Role in Games
sections.content:
"No substitute for experience, huh?"
~~Yoshihiro; Warriors Orochi

~~Yoshihiro; Warriors Orochi

sections.heading: Dynasty Warriors
sections.content:
A unique character since the third title, Huang Gai faithfully serves under three generations of the Sun family. He typically appears alongside his lord Sun Jian throughout both the Yellow Turban Rebellion and the Campaign against Dong Zhuo. He continues to fight for Wu after his lord's death by serving in Sun Ce campaign for Wu and Sun Quan's battles against Huang Zu. At the Battle of Chi Bi, he is given command of a task force whose goal is to set the Wei fleet aflame under the guise of a defection. His success allows the smaller armies of Wu and Liu Bei to inflict a devastating defeat Cao Cao's seemingly overwhelming forces.

His Legend Mode in Dynasty Warriors 4: Xtreme Legends takes place sometime before the showdown at Chi Bi. Sun Quan desires to gain an additional advantage against Cao Cao's fleet so Huang Gai volunteers to partake in a secret plan. Using deception, Zhou Yu orders a torturous beating for the Wu general and tells him to be taken away. Huang Gai feigns distrust for Wu and escapes to sneak into the enemy main camp. With his wounds still fresh, he needs to succeed in his goal while dodging the watchful eyes of other Wu generals.

Dynasty Warriors 5 states that he was a man born in poverty who trained himself to be one of the best warriors of his time. When Sun Jian issued a draft, Huang Gai joined immediately and proved himself during the Yellow Turban Rebellion. He joins his lord when they face Liu Biao and, when Sun Jian falls victim to an ambush, he encourages Sun Ce to protect the Sun family. In time, Sun Ce dies so Huang Gai serves Sun Quan. He is in charge of protecting the task force at Chi Bi. After Wu deals with the Nanman tribe and Wei, he joins the campaign to crush Shu at Bai Di Castle. He is entrusted with attacking the castle from the west and solving the Stone Sentinel Maze that guards the castle's rear. With Wu as the land's rulers in his ending, he performs a toast to his previous masters' graves with Sun Quan.

He shares his Legend Mode in the Xtreme Legends expansion with Sun Jian and Sun Ce in an the incident takes place before Huang Gai has joined the Sun family. He spots Sun Jian trying to suppress Ou Xing's rebellion and concocts a plan to open the castle's gates from the inside by using stealth. If he succeeds, he will burst the gates open and invite Sun Jian to slay Ou Xing. Huang Gai will agree to serve Sun Jian if the two meet in battle. Should his plan fail, he will alert the enemy army and will need to defeat every general on the field to open the gates.

Huang Gai's ending scene in Dynasty Warriors: Online has him and the player view their peaceful surroundings. Upon inquiring the player's intent to care for the land once his generation ends, the elderly warrior is pleased by his friend's gesture to do so.

Huang Gai has no personal story in Dynasty Warriors 6, but he continues to appear in the key battles mentioned in his infobox. When it is discovered, Huang Gai is the one who excitingly identifies the Imperial Seal for the Sun family. During Wei's version of the Battle of Chi Bi, his fire attack will succeed regardless of the players' attempts to thwart it; if they defeat Zhou Yu, Zhuge Liang, and Pang Tong, they will only weaken the tactic's effects. In Lu Bu's version of Chi Bi, it is possible to prevent Huang Gai from launching the fire attack, by either defeating him before the ship reaches Cao Cao's fleet, or by defeating Zhuge Liang before he can call out the winds. If Huang Gai is defeated, he will comment on how he can't send out the boat, and would lower the morale of the Allied forces. If he is still present when Zhou Yu and Dian Wei agree to a truce, Huang Gai will turn to attack Lu Bu's army.

Like his other appearances, Huang Gai participates in Sun Jian's campaigns and can be seen throughout the earlier sections of Wu's Story Mode in Dynasty Warriors 7. He may not always understand Sun Jian's methods, but he believes in his lord regardless. He offers comforting support for his lord's confused sons. He lives to call Sun Ce and Sun Quan as "Lord", respectively omitting his previous "Young Master". At Chibi, Zhou Yu gives him the task of setting the boats on fire, this time by ramming his single boat straight into Cao Cao's fleet. During the confusion of Cao Cao's troops, Huang Gai charges in alone to thrash his way through Cao Cao's army. He tries to pursue the fleeing commander himself but is stopped by Zhuge Liang, who assures that Liu Bei's army can take care of the rest. A wall of fire blocks Huang Gai from following, and he regretfully reports Zhuge Liang's actions to Zhou Yu. After performing his famous deed at Chibi, Huang Gai is quietly written out of the story.

His first Legendary Battle has Huang Gai, pound some discipline into the newer recruits in Sun Quan's army. His second Legendary Battle has him fight against veteran warriors found throughout the entire land.

Huang Gai appears in the second chapter of Dynasty Warriors Next where he accompanies his lord at Sishui Gate. He suspects one of the coalition members of jealousy when their supplies fail to arrive. The sixth chapter has him serve as one of Sun Quan's pillars of support. Pointing out his lord's best qualities, Huang Gai's words of encouragement help inspire the former's vision for the land. He is once again playable in the Battle of Chibi, but this time the player must have him deceive Wang Lang into opening the base needed to set the enemy's fleet on fire. In Wei's final chapter, he stands against the forces of Cao Cao to protect Jianye only to lose.

In Dynasty Warriors 8, Huang Gai reprises his role from the previous game. In the hypothetical route, he helps Lu Xun in defending Jiangxia from Wang Yi, making amends with Guan Yu and Zhang Fei.

In the Xtreme Legends expansion, Huang Gai is playable in one of Wu's alternate stages. In this scenario, he works with Sun Jian and Han Dang to attack Cao Cao's forces at Shouchun while the main force is busy fighting Yuan Shao.

Huang Gai's role as a saboteur is reprised in the Chibi campaign of Dynasty Warriors: Unleashed. He is fought two times, once during a trial set by Zhou Yu to test the player and another for his feigned defection. Officers unaware of the fake betrayal plot will rush to protect Huang Gai, forcing the player to put them down as well.

His bond story focuses on his devotion to the Sun family. When Sun Jian is killed by Huang Zu's forces, he discourages Sun Ce from recklessly retaliating and instead negotiate for a ceasefire in hopes of getting a better chance for vengeance next time. Later on at Sun Quan's behest, he successfully pacifies Shicheng by executing the corrupt officials responsible for sowing discord within the region.

During the events leading up to Chibi, Huang Gai personally gives Zhou Yu the idea of falsely defecting to Cao Cao in order to set his fleet on fire. Enduring public torture to fool everyone, he pulls the plan off and escapes with his life thanks to Han Dang's timely assistance.

In Dynasty Warriors 9, he retains his previous roles, starting as one of his lord's earliest vassals and serving him against the Yellow Turbans and Dong Zhuo. When Sun Jian's forces end up becoming a vassals of Yuan Shu, Huang Gai accompanies Sun Ce in the battle against Liu Biao. Unexpectedly, Sun Jian is slain, and the rest of his forces are forced to withdraw.

Though he and Han Dang are unwilling to become Yuan Shu's subordinates, Cheng Pu convinces them to stay and watch over the rest of the Sun Family. He later assists Sun Ce in seizing the lands of Wu for himself as well as aiding Sun Quan in taking Xiakou before Cao Cao can march south.

Once the fighting against Cao Cao's forces breaks out, Huang Gai immediately lashes out against his fellow officers as part of his ruse and is beaten by the Wu forces. After which, he will request the player for more supplies for his ship's fire attack. Although Cai He and Cai Zhong become suspicious, Huang Gai puts them at ease by claiming that his ship is filled with stolen supplies. He later uses these supplies to fuel his fire attack against the enemy fleet.

After the battle, conflict arises between Sun Quan and Liu Bei over the territory of Jing. To prevent this from escalating, Zhou Yu offers to have Sun Shangxiang marry Liu Bei. Once he is in Jianye, however, Zhou Yu attempts to kill him, but Huang Gai and the other Wu officers immediately help Liu Bei escape. Despite this attempt, Zhou Yu is pardoned, as he was only acting in Wu's interest. Though thankful, Zhou Yu quietly passes away shortly after the incident.

Though in mourning, Huang Gai decides to honor his friend by helping train Wu's younger recruits. He ends up combating Lu Xun, Zhu Ran, Xu Sheng, and Ding Feng simultaneously. Though outnumbered, Huang Gai easily overpowers the four individually, and dares them to come at him, promising to carve out the spirit and guts needed to enter the battlefield.

After his ending, his lord proceeds to occupy Ruxukou from Cao Cao's forces. Once they do so, Sun Quan launches a catastrophic attack on Hefei in conjunction with Liu Bei's attack on Hanzhong. After Taishi Ci dies from his wounds, Huang Gai and Cheng Pu succumb to illness, and Cao Cao opts to instead focus his efforts in crushing Wu at Ruxukou.

During Wei's perspective of Chibi, his false defection can be detected. If he is caught out, the fire attack will not be as strong and his defeat will cause some of the Wu soldiers to panic.

In Origins, Huang Gai's father was formerly a governor of Nanyang before his family hit hard times after his death. Shifting from one lord to another, Huang Gai found his place among Sun Jian's retainers, with the veteran idolizing his lord for his character, military prowess and political power. Huang Gai would join his lord's forces when they are tasked with subjugating both the Yellow Turbans. Later on he also helps his lord push Dong Zhuo's forces West of Luoyang. Suspected of harboring imperial ambitions and being in possession of the coveted imperial seal, Sun Jian leaves the alliance in disgust but is attacked by Liu Biao during his return journey.

Seeing a new hostile political landscape forming between Yuan Shao and Yuan Shu, Sun Jian sides with the latter and attacks Liu Biao, ordering Huang Gai and Ziluan to keep watch over both Sun Ce and Sun Shangxiang as they move towards Liu Biao at Xiangyang. During the battle, however, a fog appears and manipulates Huang Gai's subconscious jealousy of Sun Jian's prestige, delaying his arrival when Sun Jian is ambushed and killed by Huang Zu's forces.

Without their lord, Huang Gai and Sun Jian's other veterans becomes servants of Yuan Shu and are split from Sun Ce as they are ordered to do Yuan Shu's bidding from Yang Province. When Sun Ce barters the freedom of himself and his men with the Imperial Seal, Huang Gai and the other veterans are relieved to join Sun Ce's campaigns against the forces in the Wu Territory, conquering the region and establishing their own kingdom. With Cao Cao growing increasingly powerful, Huang Gai decides to suppress the local Shanyue tribes with softer tactics and diplomacy compared to Sun Ce's heavy handed tactics. Following Sun Ce's death, Huang Gai openly criticizes Sun Quan for following his brother's reckless methods in dealing with the tribes, and manages to convince some of the Shanyue to stand down to Sun Quan before their leaders eventually decide to swear nominal fealty to the Sun family.

With the local matters finally resolved, Huang Gai would participate in the campaigns against Huang Zu in Xiakou before Sun Quan forms an alliance with Liu Bei against Cao Cao. Hoping to use a fire attack against Cao Cao, Huang Gai is ordered to act suspiciously by Zhou Yu, with the mission being concealed even from both Ziluan and Sun Quan. Following through, Huang Gai allows Cai Mao to escape Sanjiangkou alive, with Zhou Yu using the event to sow suspicion in Cao Cao's forces, leading to Cai Mao's wrongful execution for suspected treason. Huang Gai is also seen suspiciously after the event, with many other officers suspecting him of dual loyalties. When he sarcastically reveals his open jealousy of Sun Jian and his frustrations with Sun Quan, Zhou Yu has Huang Gai beaten and imprisoned.

Following this incident, Huang Gai slips out of the Wu camp and joins Cao Cao's forces for the decisive battle at Chibi. He initially joins the front lines, where his presence disrupts the Wu formation, battling against Zhou Yu's forces himself. On Wei's side, the pair will individually retreat before Huang Gai returns from the Wei camp. As soon as he reaches the central galley, however, he sets it aflame, quickly spreading fire to the entire fleet and blocking a critical chokepoint for Cao Cao's retreat. If Guo Jia's fate was altered, it is possible to intercept Huang Gai and defeat him before he can launch his fire attack. On the allied side, hi role is identical except that he will wait on the western ships until the wind changes direction before launching the fire attack.

After returning back to Sun Quan's side, the young lord asks him if he will truly ever reach his brother and father's heights, but Huang Gai urges his lord to have more confidence in himself and to strive to lead in his own capacity instead of following the examples of others.

sections.heading: Warriors Orochi
sections.content:
The first game has Huang Gai make several appearances throughout Wei's storyline. He is a leader of a small anti-Orochi squadron and constantly searches for his lord's whereabouts. Although he is released each time he is captured by Cao Pi, Huang Gai is not convinced by the latter's motives and refuses to join him. Hearing that Sun Jian was being held at Odawara Castle, he falls victim to an illusion conjured by Da Ji. The phantom appears in his lord's image and Huang Gai finds himself unable to defend himself against it. After he is rescued, he realizes Cao Pi's sincerity and joins his forces.

In Warriors Orochi 2, he is back with Wu. Huang Gai and Goemon try to clear a path for Wu's advancement to Yang Ping Gate and clash with Masamune's army at Okehazama along the way. They are aided by Xu Zhu and Pang De. He shares his dream mode with two other loyal servants to their masters, Hanzō and Xu Zhu. They band together to save Gracia and aid the besieged castle's defense. When she returns, she brings Sun Jian to the field who congratulates Huang Gai's bravery.

Continuing his service to Sun Jian, Huang Gai leaves Wu to pacify various sections of the dimension realm from the serpent army's forces. Within the original timeline in Warriors Orochi 3, he joined forces with Jia Xu and mistook the base at Dongkou as an Orochi outpost. He and his comrades learn the error of their ways too late as they lose their lives against the massive serpent army that appeared later. A future Hanbei returns to the past to prevent their advance and show them the truth. To make amends for his assault, Huang Gai joins the coalition for humanity. He supports their plan to save Odawara Castle and Lianshi.

Still a loyal servant of his lord in 4, Huang Gai is sent to Honnōji with Lu Meng and Han Dang to help defend the area from Athena's forces before the reinforcements join the Coalition. He later assists in rescuing Jiang Wei's forces at Xu Province. When Ares kills Zeus and Odin emerges with Orochi X, Huang Gai and the humans help the Toyotomi army escape from Chibi.

In one of the side stories, he is among the magically possessed forces of Sekigahara, and sides with Ieyasu's army. He is beaten back into senses by Lu Meng, and joins the general in fighting Kiyomori, the source of the magic.

sections.heading: Character Information
sections.content: Huang Gai's Shin Sangoku Musou 15th Anniversary artwork.

sections.heading: Personality
sections.content:
Huang Gai is a muscular veteran who never loses faith in his countrymen. With stamina that belies his age, he is a bold and confident general for Wu. A devoted vassal, he is considered to be an irreplaceable member of the Wu army. Like Huang Zhong, he is known to make quips regarding younger warriors, but he also encourages them to always act for the greater cause. Aside from Sun Ce, he gives tips to Zhou Tai on what it means to be a loyal servant. Fond of explosions, he often mentions "booms" or "blasts" in his conversations, while also being a bit of a playful person who knows when to loosen up the mood.

While he respects each of his masters, he fondly calls Sun Jian "Great lord" (お殿, Otono).

Later games flesh out his relationships with Sun Jian's other subordinates, acting as the more light-hearted and active foil to the stoic Cheng Pu and calmer Han Dang.

During the Warriors Orochi series, he is still curious at what Jia Xu has in mind, believing him and the other strategists to be too eccentric at times. He is touched by Ginchiyo's surprising courtesy and is flattered to learn that he reminds her of her father. He gets along with Yoshihiro, and the two are invigorated by the other's strength in spite of their age. The fourth installment has him bashfully tell his stories to Fu Xi, who is intrigued of the veteran's experiences.

sections.heading: Character Symbolism
sections.content:
In the Japanese version of Dynasty Warriors 4: Empires, Huang Gai is given the nickname of "Gentle Heart Cyclops" while the English version changes it to "The Master of Martial Arts". As a dominant ruler in Dynasty Warriors 5: Empires, he calls his five greatest warriors "Huang's Five".

Huang Gai's old weapon of choice within the Dynasty Warriors series is a heavy iron whip, the same weapon he uses in Romance of the Three Kingdoms. Within ancient China, the blunt instrument was used to punish disobedience and has been alternately called a judgment whip. To disassociate with the Western interpretation of the latter weapon, however, most English translations usually refer to it as a club, mace, or rod. The addition of his bombs are likely an exaggerated homage for him setting fire to Cao Cao's fleet at Chi Bi. He historically used fire arrows for the attack, which is why he excels with bows in Dynasty Tactics and Romance of the Three Kingdoms. Though he no longer uses explosives in his sixth Dynasty Warriors appearance, Huang Gai's skill chart in Dynasty Warriors 6 is shaped in their honor.

All of his weapons' names within the Dynasty Warriors series imply that his arms have the power to stop anything dead in its tracks. His Level 11 weapon literally allowed him to break the entire world, while his third and forth whips granted him the power to halt the flow of rivers or oceans. His clubs in Dynasty Warriors 6 continue the naming trend of him wielding a superb force though they imply a greater magnitude than his previous weapons. His Standard club can sever a great river, his Skill version can slice the very sky, and his Power variation can chop through mountain peaks. Alternatively, the river mentioned for his Standard weapon doubles as a reference to the Yangtze River, said to have been a part of the stage for Chibi.

The original name for his Personal Item in Warriors Orochi is the "Wine of Sun Wu". Mentions of Sun Quan honoring his vassals with a goblet of wine at banquets is prevalent throughout historical accounts for Wu's reputed officers, including Zhou Tai and Dong Xi, and it was a high honor for those who were toasted. Huang Gai, however, was not among those who received such appraisal; perhaps the team of Omega Force sought to reward him with similar praise. As a side note, Sun Quan in the novel frequently lavishes himself with the tonic and banquets when not at war, a distorted and negative trait associated with his persona.

sections.heading: Quotes
sections.content:
- "Young punks, you still have a lot to learn!"

- "May the heavens bear witness! This fire burns with the spirit of Wu!"

- "Calling me a veteran is just a polite way of saying I'm old. Still, I'd like to see someone younger than me accomplish half of what I can do!"

- "I'm going now! This shall be the greatest moment in my life!"

- "Now, begin the fire attack! Once the flames catch, victory will be ours"

- "Burn! Burn it all! Winds! Carry the flames of Wu to the sky!"

- "Justice is with me! Prepare yourself!"

- "Without you my strategies wouldn't work. I thank you."

- "The young'uns have been getting a lot stronger lately."

- "Master Huang Gai. You've achieved success in battle many times, and I presume been first into battle too?"

- "My lord... Where are you, my lord...?"

- "Even with Orochi dead, will your evil never stop?"

- "Master Huang Gai, your physique is most impressive. It is hard to believe it belongs to an old warrior such as yourself."

- "Ah, Huang Gai! You're making a name for yourself. Watching your youth in action feels great! Hahaha!"

See also: Huang Gai/Quotes

~~Dynasty Warriors 4

~~Dynasty Warriors 4

~~Dynasty Warriors 4

~~Dynasty Warriors 4

"What? Jaundice? Then you should be in bed, kid!"
~~Ma Chao and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors 5

~~Ma Chao and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors 5

"Sometimes I would like to just cut loose. But completing difficult assignments is the pride of the aged."

"Hah! Is that so? Then perhaps I should give you all the really difficult work."

"Heheheh. I look forward to it!"
~~Zhou Yu and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors 7

~~Zhou Yu and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors 7

"Yeah, they're all standing out. It must be nice to be noticed just because you're young..."

"But it can be dangerous to rush forward so fast. We must continue to set a good example for them."

"An example, huh... That'll only work if they actually pay attention though..."
~~Huang Gai and Han Dang; Dynasty Warriors 8

~~Huang Gai and Han Dang; Dynasty Warriors 8

"Of course. That kind of dangerous work is exactly something for an old veteran like myself."

"What are you saying, Master Huang Gai? That you're willing to sacrifice yourself so readily?"

"Sacrifice? Don't you understand that the first one into battle is a vital role? One that can't afford to fail? If the frontlines break down then the enemy will rush through and put all of our allies in danger. Be mindful."

"M-My apologies. Being the first into battle is clearly far more than just a way to show off."
~~Yue Jin and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors: Godseekers

~~Yue Jin and Huang Gai; Dynasty Warriors: Godseekers

"Sun Jian is blessed with faithful retainers."

"Ieyasu, what should I do?"

"Above all, you must stay calm."
~~Huang Gai and Ieyasu; Warriors Orochi

~~Huang Gai and Ieyasu; Warriors Orochi

"Evil? Hah, such childish, clean-cut, schoolboy morals are outdated, old man."
~~Huang Gai and Masamune; Warriors Orochi 2

~~Huang Gai and Masamune; Warriors Orochi 2

"As long as I remain on the battlefield, I must keep pace with these younger guys! Master Shimazu, you wield that enormous war hammer in battle. Your build must also be quite impressive under that armor."
~~Yoshihiro and Huang Gai; Warriors Orochi 3

~~Yoshihiro and Huang Gai; Warriors Orochi 3

"Please, Master Fu Xi, you mock me! I'm sure there are actual young people out there far more promising than me."

"Well, everyone has their unique qualities, that's for sure. But we are here to observe, not lead. That's for you to do."

"Why? You are right here with us, Master Fu Xi. Wouldn't it be better for everyone to benefit from your experience?"

"That isn't how this works. The world of humans needs each generation to pass correctly to the next. You lead the youth, and set an example to follow. Those who see you grow, come to lead, and eventually pass that torch themselves. That unbroken chain is what makes the human world so precious."
~~Fu Xi and Huang Gai; Warriors Orochi 4

~~Fu Xi and Huang Gai; Warriors Orochi 4

sections.heading: Dynasty Warriors 9
sections.content:
『真・三國無双８』黄蓋アクション動画

Huang Gai is affiliated with the club in this title. When equipped with it, Huang Gai can perform the following unique attacks listed below.

Unique Flow Attack Finisher: Enemy Downed +    : Plants club's head on the floor and uses it to perform a swift pole vault kick with both feet aimed forward.

Unique Trigger Attack: R1 + : Sends foes airborne by crushing the ground hard. If charged, this move will cause targets to spiral even higher in the air.

Special Technique: R1 + : Generates an updraft of wind while lifting club in a crouched manner, then throws it down on the floor to unleash a radial explosion.

Musou Attack: : Lunges at a nearby opponent and grabs them by the legs, performing continuous giant swings that also hit nearby foes. After hurling his victim away, Huang Gai bear hugs another foe and sends them flying high in time for him to jump up and catch them in mid-air for a vicious back-breaking bend. The final motion also causes flames to briefly erupt around him for added effect. Based on his Backbreaker Musou attack from past titles.

Aerial Musou Attack:  : Performs a brainbuster on a single enemy while dropping down with enough impact to knock away other surrounding foes and setting them ablaze at the same time. If the attack fails to connect, Huang Gai will instead do a fiery lariat punch to knock over potential targets. Derived from Huang Gai's Mountain Drop Musou attack in the eighth installment.

Summon Horse: L2: Summon horse to the battlefield. Holding L2 as the horse approaches will allow the character to automatically mount them.

Auto-Run: Riding Mode + L2: Automatically runs towards a specified destination. If a destination has not been set, the horse will instead run towards the location of the player's currently selected mission.

Sprint: Riding Mode + Direction + R1: Causes horse to run at a faster pace. Doing so depletes their stamina each time.

Jump: Riding Mode + Direction + R2: Causes horse to jump high while running.

Horse Fast Attack: Riding Mode + : Performs a fast and seamless attack while mounted. The attack itself varies depending on what type of weapon is equipped and whether the enemy is grounded or airborne.

Horse Trigger Attack: Riding Mode + : Performs an attack that sends enemies flying into the air. May vary depending on what type of weapon is equipped.

Horse Musou Attack: Riding Mode + : Causes horse to sprint by consuming Musou instead of stamina.

Dismount Horse: Riding Mode + : Dismounts from horse and resumes default fighting stance.

Shooting Mode: 🡻: Pulls out bow and assumes archery stance. Can be cancelled by tapping 🡻 again.

Switch Arrows: 🡸 or 🡺: Switches between different types of arrows.

Bow Attack: Shooting Mode + : Fires current arrow at a target. Can lock on targets by tapping R3.

Lower Bow: Shooting Mode + : Lowers bow while squatting down.

sections.heading: Historical Information
sections.content:
Huang Gai originated from the Quanling county in the Lingling precinct. He was orphaned at an early age, and endured many difficulties as a baby. Even in his poor situation, he retained great ambition and studied how to read and write, and discussed military affairs.

He started off his career as a minor clerk within a commandery office where he received a  recommendation as "Filial and Incorrupt", an honor bestowed on talented people who retain filial piety and upright behavior. When Sun Jian was gathering a volunteer force for the war against Dong Zhuo, Huang Gai joined him. He was made a major with his own command after the defeat of the Shanyue bandits and Dong Zhuo's retreat in the north. Following Sun Jian's death, Huang Gai continued to serve Wu under Jian's sons.

The Shanyue were rebellious, and Huang Gai was assigned to oversee the crippled prefectures. The officers in Shifeng were insubordinate and difficult to control, so Huang Gai appointed two of them as heads of departments. He said to them, "Being the county's magistrate, I am neither talented nor virtuous. I obtained this post based on military merits, and administrative affairs are not my specialty. At this time, the bandits have not been pacified, and I have military duties to fulfill. Thus, I delegate the two of you to be in charge of all the documents, to supervise the various departments, and to point out as well as correct any mistake that they make. Within the jurisdictions of your two offices, you should discharge your responsibility for your administrative duties properly. Though I will neither punish you with whip nor cane, I hope that you will perform your duties to the best of your abilities, and not set a bad example for the rest." As a result of the speech, the officers began taking their tasks seriously.

After seeing that Huang Gai did not check their documents, however, the two department heads eventually became relaxed and lenient. Huang Gai grew disgusted at their laziness, and obtained evidence of their unlawful activities. He then held a gathering, inviting all officers to a banquet. During the meal, he brought out the evidence and interrogated the two officials. The department heads had nothing to say in their defense, and begged for forgiveness. Huang Gai said, "Some time ago, I had already set out the decree – ‘Nor whip nor cane will I lay on you’. That was not said in vain." He ordered the two officers to be executed, sending fear throughout the prefecture.

Later, Huang Gai was assigned to be Prefect of Xunyang and Chief of Chunju, and in all was put in charge of nine prefectures. Wherever he commanded, the land became peaceful. In Danyang, Huang Gai received support from the Shanyue because he had supported the weak and put down the powerful. He was said to have looks that were stern and determined, while excelling at training soldiers. Every time a military expedition was called, his troops would fight to go first.

He followed Zhou Yu to defend against Cao Cao at Chibi, and notably conceived the stratagem of a fire attack. At the battle, Huang Gai was hit by a stray arrow and fell into a cold river. Some Wu soldiers rescued him and placed him on a bed, but did not recognize him as Huang Gai. He gathered enough strength to call out Han Dang's name, and when Han Dang heard it, immediately turned to him and assisted him to change his clothing. Huang Gai recovered and the campaign at Chibi was a success.

When the southern tribes of Wuling began rebelling, Huang Gai assumed the duties of the Grand Administrator. Having only five hundred men at his command and knowing they would lose in a savage attack, he had the city gates opened, allowing half of the rebels to come inside. His army then struck, quickly defeating several hundred enemies. The rest fled, returning to their respective towns. Huang Gai then defeated the leader of the rebels and pardoned those who surrendered. Between spring and summer, all of the local rebellions were pacified and peace was brought to the region.

Huang Gai later died of illness in office after being promoted to Lieutenant-General. He was posthumously recognized as one of the Five Elders of Wu. People in the kingdom remembered him fondly, offering sacrifices to him all year long. He was succeeded by his son, Huang Bing.

sections.heading: Romance of the Three Kingdoms
sections.content:
In the novel Romance of Three Kingdoms, Zhuge Liang is credited with devising the fire attack instead of Huang Gai. As part of the ruse, Huang Gai allows himself to be flogged severely by Zhou Yu in front of the other generals to fool Cao Cao into accepting his false defection. The plan is a success, resulting in Wei's humiliating downfall at the battle of Chibi.

- Warriors series

- Romance of the Three Kingdoms
